skill.name: main-orchestrator
skill.description: Decomposes broad product requirements into concrete tasks and coordinates frontend, backend, data, test, and devops orchestrators.
skill.summary: Plan multi-domain engineering work, assign tasks to specialist orchestrators, and track delivery through project state files.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed lines are markdown examples, inline file names, or ordinary prose. No executable code, network calls, credential handling, prompt injection attempt, or cryptographic implementation was found; the main residual risk is intended local file reads and writes for orchestration state.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple markdown code fences and inline backtick file references as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines document YAML, JSON, and file paths; they are not executable skill code and do not create command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are fenced markdown examples or inline references in SKILL.md. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or executable command invocation at those lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings correspond to ordinary skill description text and example field names, not hashing or encryption code. No use of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related cryptographic APIs was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain frontmatter text and YAML or JSON example fields. They do not implement or call any cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File State Updates
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read and update local coordination files, including operations logs, task state, and architecture decisions. This is expected for an orchestrator, but users should apply it only in repositories where the agent may modify planning state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicitly documented and scoped to project state files. They are a legitimate capability, with minor risk from unintended workspace changes.

content.value_statement: Complex feature requests often span frontend, backend, data, testing, and deployment work. This skill turns broad requirements into coordinated tasks with dependencies, owners, checks, and progress tracking.
actual_capabilities: Breaks product requirements into epics, tasks, dependencies, and success criteria.
Assigns work to frontend, backend, data, test, and devops orchestrators.
Defines validation checks for happy path, error, invalid, edge, functional, visual, performance, and security scenarios.
Coordinates conflicts such as API misalignment, circular dependencies, and resource constraints.
Tracks progress through operations logs, task state, and architecture decision records.
limitations: It provides orchestration guidance, not direct implementation of application code.
It assumes compatible domain orchestrator skills and project state files exist.
It does not replace human product prioritization or final architecture approval.
It can create inaccurate plans when requirements are incomplete or ambiguous.

use_cases.title: Break down a new product feature
use_cases.description: Convert a PRD into coordinated frontend, backend, data, test, and devops tasks with clear dependencies.

use_cases.title: Coordinate cross-team engineering delivery
use_cases.description: Route work to specialist agents and maintain task status across a multi-domain implementation effort.

use_cases.title: Prepare implementation plans for agents
use_cases.description: Create task packets with acceptance checks, required standards, ownership, and completion criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a small feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one clear requirement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use main-orchestrator to decompose this feature request into tasks, dependencies, owners, and acceptance checks: [feature request].

prompt_templates.title: Turn a PRD into an epic
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning from a product document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use main-orchestrator to analyze this PRD and produce an epic structure with domain contexts, task sequence, quality gates, and success criteria: [PRD].

prompt_templates.title: Resolve delivery conflicts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced coordination when teams or agents are blocked.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use main-orchestrator to review these tasks, identify cross-domain conflicts or circular dependencies, and propose a revised execution order: [task list].

prompt_templates.title: Run full orchestration planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use main-orchestrator to create an end-to-end delivery plan for this initiative. Include task decomposition, orchestrator assignments, dependencies, validation checks, progress events, and documentation triggers: [initiative].

output_examples.input: Add OAuth2 login with Google and GitHub.
output: Epic for OAuth2 login with high priority.
Frontend task for login buttons and user flow.
Backend task for provider integration and token handling.
Data task for account linking storage.
Test task for end-to-end OAuth validation.

output_examples.input: Frontend needs an API that backend has not built yet.
output: Create a frontend mock API task.
Update the backend task with the required contract.
Add a dependency from frontend completion to backend completion.
Record the architecture decision for later review.

output_examples.input: Several critical tasks compete for limited agent capacity.
output: Rank tasks by business impact and risk.
Serialize the highest impact work first.
Document delay reasoning in task notes.

best_practices: Provide complete requirements, affected domains, known constraints, and desired success metrics before planning.
Review generated task dependencies before allowing agents to modify project state files.
Keep operations logs and task status files current during execution.
anti_patterns: Using it for a single isolated task that does not need orchestration.
Skipping acceptance checks because the implementation seems simple.
Letting the plan modify project state without reviewing unclear requirements.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It converts broad software requirements into epics, tasks, dependencies, owners, checks, and progress events.

faq.question: Does it write application code?
faq.answer: No. It plans and coordinates work across domain orchestrators that may perform implementation tasks.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require other skills?
faq.answer: It is designed to coordinate frontend, backend, data, test, devops, and documentation orchestrator skills.

faq.question: What files can it affect?
faq.answer: It may read or update local planning files such as operations logs, task state, and architecture decision records.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious code or executable command behavior, with only low filesystem coordination risk.
